quotes: Code is the architecture of thoughts translated into action.
Keep it simple, but significant.
Failures are just feedback on the path to better ideas.
Simplicity is the ultimate sophistication.
Refactor often; the code you touch today becomes the future you thank tomorrow.
Measure twice, code once.
Iterate fast, learn faster.
Good ideas are useless without good execution.
Comfort with ambiguity is a feature, not a bug.
Write code for humans first, machines second.
Make it work, then make it nice.
If it hurts, fix the bottleneck.
Tests are the safety rails of invention.
Documentation is a love letter to the future you.
Patterns exist to be understood, not worshipped.
Performance without clarity is a hidden bottleneck.
Quality is a feature, not a bug.
Security is baked in, not bolted on.
Latency is a user experience issue, not a metric.
Conversations with code teach us more than code teaches us.
Code is poetry when it reads like music to the mind.
Consistency beats cleverness every time.
Better abstractions today prevent bigger problems tomorrow.
Seek feedback early; breakages in the morning save hours later.
Less code, fewer bugs, longer maintainability.
Design for testing; test for design.
Dream big, ship small, document everything.
Empathy for future maintainers is the best optimization.
Be stubborn about details; flexible about approaches.
Open source, open minds, open futures.
